CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 17054026) FILED BY DEFENDANT VIANT TECHNOLOGY LLC VIANT TECHNOLOGY HOLDING INC. TIME INC. TI NEWCO LLC ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 3.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VIANT TECHNOLOGY LLC, VIANT TECHNOLOGY HOLDING INC., TIME INC., TI NEWCO LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO BOTH THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR COMMON COUNTS. PLAINTIFF BITTORRENT, INC. ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT AN ALTER EGO THEORY OF LIABILITY AGAINST THE VANDERHOOK DEFENDANTS. BITTORRENT ALLEGES SEVERAL OF THE FACTORS IDENTIFIED IN ASSOCIATED VENDORS, INC. V. OAKLAND MEAT CO. (1962) 210 CAL.APP.2D 825, 838-841 WHICH SHOW UNITY OF INTEREST AND THAT THE VANDERHOOKS MANIPULATED THE VARIOUS ENTITIES TO ESCAPE LIABILITY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

AMENDED PROOF OF SERVICE RE: REPLY TO PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER (TRANSACTION ID # 17078038) FILED BY DEFENDANT VIANT TECHNOLOGY LLC VIANT TECHNOLOGY HOLDING INC. TIME INC. TI NEWCO LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TIMOTHY VANDERHOOK, CHRISTOPHER VANDERHOOK, RUSSELL VANDERHOOK'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR COMMON COUNTS AND SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD. PLAINTIFF BITTORRENT, INC. ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT AN ALTER EGO THEORY OF LIABILITY AGAINST THE VANDERHOOK DEFENDANTS. BITTORRENT ALLEGES SEVERAL OF THE FACTORS IDENTIFIED IN ASSOCIATED VENDORS, INC. V. OAKLAND MEAT CO. (1962) 210 CAL.APP.2D 825, 838-841 WHICH SHOW UNITY OF INTEREST AND THAT THE VANDERHOOKS MANIPULATED THE VARIOUS ENTITIES TO ESCAPE LIABILITY. THE ALLEGATION IN PARAGRAPH 20 OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT THE DEFENDANTS PAID THE INVOICES FOR 3 MONTHS APPEARS TO BE INCONSISTENT WITH THE ALLEGATION IN THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION THAT THE VANDERHOOKS DID NOT INTEND TO PAY BITTORENT. ABSENT SOME FURTHER EXPLANATION, THE ALLEGATION OF PARTIAL PERFORMANCE DEFEATS THE FRAUD CLAIM. IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, BITTORENT IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND ITS FRAUD CLAIM TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THE VANDERHOOKS' LACK OF INTENTION TO PAY BITTORENT NOTWITHSTANDING THAT SEVERAL INVOICES WERE PAID. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-03-2017 Continued To Jun-21-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Crystal N. Riggins, of Hoge Fenton Attorneys, Tel. 408.287.9501, for plaintiffs Bittorent, Inc.;
Timothy S. Fox, of LTL Attorneys LLP, Tel. 213.612.8900, for defendants Viant Technology LLC, Viant Technology Holding, Inc., TI Newco LLC, and Time Inc.;
T. Kevin Roosevelt, of Finlayson Toffer Roosevelt & Lilly LLP, Tel. 949.759.3810, for defendants Timothy Vanderhook, Russell Vanderhook & Christopher Vanderhook.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TIMOTHY VANDERHOOK, CHRISTOPHER VANDERHOOK, RUSSELL VANDERHOOK's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to the first cause of action for breach of contract and the second cause of action for common counts and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the third cause of action for fraud. Plaintiff Bittorrent, Inc. alleges sufficient facts to support an alter ego theory of liability against the Vanderhook defendants. Bittorrent alleges several of the factors identified in Associated Vendors, Inc. v. Oakland Meat Co. (1962) 210 Cal.App.2d 825, 838-841 which show unity of interest and that the Vanderhooks manipulated the various entities to escape liability. The allegation in paragraph 20 of the second amended complaint that the defendants paid the invoices for 3 months appears to be inconsistent with the allegation in the fraud cause of action that the Vanderhooks did not intend to pay Bittorent. Absent some further explanation, the allegation of partial performance defeats the fraud claim. If it can do so in good faith, Bittorent is given leave to amend its fraud claim to allege facts showing the Vanderhooks' lack of intention to pay Bittorent notwithstanding that several invoices were paid. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Crystal N. Riggins, of Hoge Fenton Attorneys, Tel. 408.287.9501, for plaintiffs Bittorent, Inc.;
Timothy S. Fox, of LTL Attorneys LLP, Tel. 213.612.8900, for defendants Viant Technology LLC, Viant Technology Holding, Inc., TI Newco LLC, and Time Inc.;
T. Kevin Roosevelt, of Finlayson Toffer Roosevelt & Lilly LLP, Tel. 949.759.3810, for defendants Timothy Vanderhook, Russell Vanderhook & Christopher Vanderhook.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT VIANT TECHNOLOGY LLC, VIANT TECHNOLOGY HOLDING INC., TIME INC., TI NEWCO LLC's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to both the first cause of action for breach of contract and the second cause of action for common counts. Plaintiff Bittorrent, Inc. alleges sufficient facts to support an alter ego theory of liability against the Vanderhook defendants. Bittorrent alleges several of the factors identified in Associated Vendors, Inc. v. Oakland Meat Co. (1962) 210 Cal.App.2d 825, 838-841 which show unity of interest and that the Vanderhooks manipulated the various entities to escape liability. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Continued To Mar-15-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

No notice of contest was provided to the Court. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS VIANT TECHNOLOGY LLC, VIANT TECHNOLOGY HOLDING INC. AND TIME INC.'S (COLLECTIVELY "Moving Defendants) DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff BitTorrent Inc's theory of recovery against the Moving Defendants on both causes of action appears to be alter ego or successor liability/assumption of liability. But the FAC does not contain allegations sufficient to maintain a cause of action against the Moving Defendants on either theory. BitTorrent has demonstrated a reasonable possibility that the defects can be cured. BitTorrent is granted 10 days leave to amend. Order signed.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. (302/JQ)
